ABOUT.TITLE: Technical Overview
ABOUT.SUBTITLE: Final Degree Project · Multiplatform Application Development
ABOUT.HOOK: A production-ready microservices platform that aggregates external data with intelligent caching, reducing outbound API calls by over 90%.
ABOUT.ARCH_DESC: Microservices architecture with an API Gateway as the sole public entry point. The gateway validates JWT tokens, checks Redis for revoked tokens, and injects user context into headers. Services publish Kafka events consumed by ko2-kafka-events and persisted in MySQL for real-time auditing.
ABOUT.EVENT_DESC: Asynchronous audit system based on Kafka events. The three services publish events in real time, which the ko2-kafka-events microservice consumes and persists in MySQL.
ABOUT.EVENT_KAFKA: Kafka KRaft without Zookeeper — embedded broker, no external dependencies
ABOUT.EVENT_AUDIT: Real-time persistence to audit_events and alert_events · automatic rate-limiting alerts
ABOUT.SECURITY_BCRYPT: BCrypt password hashing in the database
ABOUT.SECURITY_BLACKLIST: Token blacklist in Redis with dynamic TTL — on logout, the token is invalidated server-side, eliminating orphaned tokens without cleanup jobs
ABOUT.SECURITY_RBAC: RBAC: ROLE_USER accesses weather and currency data. ROLE_ADMIN can also clear the cache
ABOUT.SECURITY_HEADERS: Identity propagation between microservices via X-User-Name and X-User-Roles headers
ABOUT.CACHE_DESC: Cascading cache system that minimizes calls to external APIs. In production, this reduces outgoing requests by over 90%.
ABOUT.CACHE_L2: MySQL (Aiven) · warm cache · if fetchedAt < 10 min → use DB data
ABOUT.CACHE_L3: External API · open.er-api.com for currencies · Open-Meteo for weather
ABOUT.CACHE_L4: Fallback · stale DB data if external API fails · stale data beats HTTP 500
ABOUT.DECISION_REDIS_DESC: The token self-expires in Redis at the same time as the JWT. No periodic cleanup jobs or purge logic required.
ABOUT.DECISION_WEBFLUX_DESC: The Gateway is purely I/O-bound: it forwards requests without computation. Internal services use Spring MVC, where reactive complexity adds no value.
ABOUT.DECISION_FALLBACK_DESC: Availability over freshness: slightly stale data is preferable to a 500 error that leaves the screen blank.
ABOUT.DECISION_HEADERS_DESC: The Gateway is the trust boundary. Internal services trust the X-User-Name and X-User-Roles headers without re-validating JWT internally.
ABOUT.DECISION_STANDALONE_DESC: Direct lazy-loading without NgModules, optimal tree-shaking, and faster builds. Aligned with the official framework direction since Angular 17.
ABOUT.DECISION_RXJS_DESC: Automatically cancels previous requests and waits 500ms before firing a search, eliminating race conditions and unnecessary API calls.
ABOUT.DECISION_KAFKA_DESC: Lightweight event broker for asynchronous auditing. KRaft removes the Zookeeper dependency, reducing the operational surface to a single process.
ABOUT.DEPLOY_BACKEND: Backend on Hetzner VPS · Docker Compose · 5 containers (gateway, auth, api, ko2-kafka-events, kafka) + Prometheus
ABOUT.DEPLOY_FRONTEND: Frontend on Vercel · global CDN · ko2-oreilly.com domain
ABOUT.DEPLOY_DB: MySQL managed on Aiven · Redis managed on Railway · no data infrastructure maintenance
ABOUT.DEPLOY_APIS: External APIs: open.er-api.com (currencies) · Open-Meteo (weather, free without API key)
